review_title: Great product, but hard to use with a multi instance saas company and can only use Firefox.
author: Steven M.
pros: Great product that integrates with a webage to provide seamless training, pretty simple to use. Great concept that uses the actual love system.
cons: Firefox is not a widely used browser anymore, and most of our clients do not have it so could not purchase it. Also, we have several instances of our product, and wanted to price out per instance making it really expensive.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: How innovative and smart it is, it makes our relationship with our customers so much easier and fluid, so many great tools and functionalities, it has very to no little limitations on what you can do
cons: A bit more difficult to learn than expected, but that's all really, never encountered any actual issues or inconveniences

review_title: Onboarding app that needs to improve
description: The potential is there, but it has many room for improvement for it to be "self served"
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It lets you build complete workflows that take users "hand in hand" until they complete a desired flow, in addition to building "one-time" helpful popups
cons: It has many bugs. Maybe it works well for simple websites, but we have a problem with almost every implementation of a new onboarding workflow we want to run on our platform, which then requires the help of their support/tech team.

description: The customer support and sales staff at WalkMe are great at helping unlock the potential benefits. We are currently considering a pilot program that I'm hopeful will take off and lead to enterprise-wide use.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: There are two things that I loved about WalkMe. 1. The overall premise of improving the user experience and guiding them at the time of need. 2. How easy it is for a non-technical person to author any type of WalkMe function.
cons: It's definitely better for form-based applications than more complicated non-linear applications. Some of the applications we wanted to use this on were too complex (at least for beginner authors) to have a meaningful impact on the end users.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is really easy to create launchers, tips, validation, and walk-thru to provide assistance to the users and assign tasks to newly hired employees to train them about the system and track their training record via reporting. We can also segregate different materials for different clients and we can also create segments for faster rule application on any item.
cons: the only con is that we cannot host any resources like videos and documents, we can only add their private links.

review_title: Ironically, it's incredibly hard to use their product
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This can be very powerful software if set up properly. We were able to do advanced tutorials, though it took professional services to set it up for us. We found it the most powerful in the market from a feature standpoint.
cons: hard to use. WalkMe is a software that makes it easy to show visitors how to use any product. Unfortunately their product is prohibitively hard to use. They have set up a "walkme university" and require 10 hours of training and watching videos just to get started. They typically give you a CSM for 3 months to help you onboard and throw in free professional services because most things are too hard to figure out.

review_title: Great for Product Walk-throughs, Mistaken as Instructional Tool
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Great amount of customization, triggers and flows are intuitive and applicable to most all use cases, easy to use and train others on.
cons: It's often mistaken and mislabeled as an "instructional" tool. Mobile is an added cost.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The most robust Self-Guidance application out there.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: -In the adoption category, it is by far the most advanced. -Good customer service and onboarding. -The customization and branding within application is possible.
cons: It depends on your size and if you have someone who can dedicate time to building out the walk-thrus post-onboarding.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I have only ever used WalkMe for customer tutorials so I don't really have anything to base this off of. I personally liked WalkMe and I think our customers did too when learning and training on our new software platforms.
cons: Sometimes I had issues getting the videos to playback but I was able to fix it with a software reboot.

review_title: Easy to use, but sometimes difficult to setup
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love how easy this software is to use for my end users. It's easy to click and follow and train them to complete tasks without needing to physically contact our support department.
cons: I dislike how picky and problematic it can be to setup. Depending on your web page's set up, it can be simple point and click to build, or require you to learn jquery to build every single step.

review_title: WalkMe un programme de formation interne
description: Le produit a été très riche en fonctionnalités et il faut dire qu’ils font un excellent leur travail. »
author: Sandrine M.
pros: L'implémentation a été excellente et nous avons pu améliorer de manière significative l'expérience utilisateur
cons: l’outil a des fonctionnalités beaucoup moins accessibles à l’utilisateur lambda disposant de connaissances techniques limitées.

description: WalkMe is a pretty simple and creative tool to use to target your audience. It has benefited us in making it much easier to target our audience to download and use our services! We have received great customer support, which has helped us every bit of the way. WalkMe is definitely worth every cent with all of the feature reach functionality you receive!
author: Vince P.
pros: WalkMe has a great resource management dashboard . Easy-to-use dashboard. I can create customized walk-throughs for our customers to utilize to learn different tasks within the software. Overall WalkMe is just a fun and easy product to use. Connecting with my customers resources feels very rewarding, and is a HUGH Benefit for us. It keeps us connected to our customers and meeting there needs.
cons: Getting implemented was a bit tricky for us. It took longer than expected probably because our business and our process in general are a bit more difficult for others to grasp. You do need to have patience because you do need to build everything from scratch but once you are up and running that is when the fun begins. At least that was the case for us.

description: Walkme has been a great tool for us as a company especially in regions where we do not have an administrator to answer questions. It's a great tool for new users in understanding not only Salesforce but company customization in salesforce.
author: Alison O.
pros: Easy to install and great for new beginners
cons: Lots of set up required on the back end and if you move fields around, won't capture those changes

description: WalkMe was used to create call outs and on-boarding materials for a SAAS platform, link to documentation, and help increase utilization of product features, in addition to utilizing NPS data gathering.
author: Cody H.
pros: WalkMe is an incredibly powerful tool that allows deep integration with your website or software.
cons: This product is very costly, putting it out of reach for most small businesses.

author: Deirdre G.
pros: It's a great tool when you're not sure on how to get through a call situation. The walkme tool is definitely an asset to my work tools.
cons: When I transfer a call, it repeats the questions after the transfer is complete. I'd like a cancel button to stop it.

author: Landrum R.
pros: Prior to implementing WalkMe my customers would ask questions then my support staff would answer them on a case by case basis. Now however, if a customer needs assistance with a particular task we create a live walk-through for them so that our library of walk-throughs is being built around customer service.
cons: I would recommend any user attempting to write engaging walk-throughs for the web to have a basic understanding of css and Jquery selectors.

description: Love this software! It's pretty simple to create walk-thrus for your target audience, along with it being even easier for your target audience to download and use! Great customer support, has helped me every bit of the way, and definitely worth every cent with all of the functionality you get!
author: Ashley D.
pros: How easy and quick it is to use the software from a user end, or a creator end.
cons: Nothing I really dislike about this software.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Darrus L.
pros: Excellent for creating educational walkthroughs for web based software. The interface is simple and easy to use. Most of the features work well and do not degrade the performance of the webpage for the end user.
cons: Functionality a little iffy at times. Apps are not capable of performing basic logical operations and are mostly locked into a linear progression with little to no handling for users who may deviate from the specified path.
author_profession: Program Manager eLearning and Modern Workplace
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great! Sets people up to succeed!
author: Pascale S.
pros: Simple. Easy to use. Really great customer service associates readily available to assist with questions.
cons: It can be a bit complicated and confusing trying to create walk-throughs for processes that have lots and lots of steps.

review_title: Guided User Support When They Need It
author: Nick T.
pros: WalkMe definitely lives up to its name. The step by step walkthroughs is great support for users when they need it. Producing the content is easy.
cons: Complex processes/steps need more expertise. My team has yet to check out all its features, but so far so good.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: I am going to writing a Walkme review because it worked well for me
description: It helps prevent us from being overwhelmed with the same monotonous questions on a daily basis. It runs 24 hours a day, so we don't need to hire employees in each time zone and it really removes the mundane tasks from our plate. It is very good for a small company such as ourselves.
author: Brion S.
pros: I've been at my desk since 11am so I am pretty much zoned out now, however Walkme is alright because it does answer people, it just copies information from our help articles and provides it to customers (so I don't have to), also I am relieved it did not break today. It is alright.
cons: The pricing model is a bit odd as you are billed by resolution and I never really know what my monthly bill will be as it can mess up sometimes and tell customers they qualify for discounts when they don't...this causes me a tremendous amount of headache to correct and I hate how it responds to all customer emails. I would love to be able to control the channel better as it seems somewhat limited.

description: We used it to make an existing software experience much better for the end user and it delivered for us.
author: Carlo L.
pros: Walkme made our previous EHR system much better.
cons: Building things in Walkme sometimes became too technical, not super easy, and required an amount of precision.

description: WalkMe has cut down on our B2B related support tickets by over 25% since we've started in 2020. We have seen great success in our implementation and look to leverage this software in other areas of our business. We aim to cut down on our in-person training needs and training time by 50% using the tool.
author: Justin M.
pros: WalkMe lives on top of a website or software program. Currently, we utilize WalkMe on our B2B website, where we can reach our dealers and sales reps who are using the site around the globe. We have built Smart Walk-Thru's that allow users to train themselves on how to use the site and learn how to navigate around it to find what they need.
cons: WalkMe requires a significant onboarding/learning process. Knowledge of coding is necessary to take full advantage of the software, otherwise you need to lean on their technical team. The team is great, but you have to pay for the support hours and they can go quickly if you have a complicated build.

description: It saves one of the things we all need most - time. Give your users the ability to train themselves. Give them the support they need to help them get to where they need to go. Alleviate the stress on your support teams so they can focus on big ticket items.
author: Adrian M.
pros: WalkMe has been useful in removing some of the daily questions support teams typically field. Simple questions on how do I access something, how do I get from point A to point B can be answered with on-screen guidance and allow your support team to focus on high priority tickets. Rolling out a new platform also becomes a bit easier and alleviates the pain of having to perform training after training.
cons: Not much I didn't like - the only piece that is tricky is creating WalkMe's across different platforms, but it is possible. The other challenge is trying to nail down some of the funnels to be more precise. However, WalkMe support teams do an amazing job in helping.

review_title: Extremely robust platform for digital adoption
description: Customer self-onboarding. Benefits: Scalability of new customer onboarding. We were able to onboard customers much faster after deploying WalkMe, allowing CS to focus on renewal and expansion with existing customers.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: WalkMe has a ton of functionality to allow you to create walk throughs and other types of dynamic user education. I love how easy it is to integrate outside content like videos, help desk articles, webpages, etc. with your custom walk throughs. Probably my favorite thing about walk me is that Customer Success and UX can use the platform to implement super detailed user education without needing to involve engineering much at all. In my experience, user education often gets deprioritized next to new feature development and bug fixes, so things like in-app onboarding and tool tips only get built when there's a bit of extra capacity, and it rarely is able to keep up with the evolution of the product. With WalkMe, the teams focused on customer success and experience can quickly implement the changes they want without involving half the company.
cons: It is a very robust platform, so learning all the functionality takes time. Unless you're a javascript and CSS pro, you will probably struggle with getting the jQuery selectors right and the CSS exactly how you want the experience to look and feel. The out-of-the box features will get you 90% of the way there though.
